title: My Rank Composite
summary: Pick your trusted ranking sources, assign weights, and see a single personalized "My Rank" column on the Free Agents page.
description: The new My Rank feature lets you build a custom composite ranking from any combination of your imported rankings. On the Import Rankings page, check the sources you trust most and assign each a weight (1x, 2x, or 3x) to control how much influence it has.

Once two or more sources are selected, a "My Rank" column appears as the first ranking column on the Free Agents page with automatic sorting. Composite member columns are grouped together with a subtle border separator so you can always see how individual sources compare to your blended rank.
imageAlt: Import Rankings page showing ranking source selection with weight controls for building a My Rank composite

title: What's New Blog Redesign
summary: Browse updates with category filters, full detail pages, and a timeline sidebar.
description: The What's New page has been redesigned as a blog-style experience. Filter entries by category using horizontal pill tabs — New Pages, New Features, and Enhancements. The most recent entry in each month is highlighted at full width.

Every entry now has its own detail page with a full description, category badge, CTA button, and prev/next navigation to browse chronologically. A sticky timeline sidebar on desktop shows month labels with color-coded dots for quick anchor navigation.
Entries you haven't seen since your last visit are marked with a NEW badge, so you always know what's fresh.
imageAlt: What's New page with category filter tabs, featured entry cards, and timeline sidebar

title: Salary Archive
summary: Browse and download every historical salary spreadsheet from 2009 to 2025.
description: A new Salary Archive page gives you access to every historical Tagged Salary Averages spreadsheet dating back to 2009. Each year links directly to the original spreadsheet file for download.

The Salary Analytics page also now features an Export to Excel button that generates a multi-sheet workbook with one tab per position, plus a Position Summary overview. Select any season and week, then export the full salary data in one click.
imageAlt: Salary Archive page listing historical salary spreadsheets from 2009 to 2025

title: Ranking Source in Roster Column
summary: The roster rank column now displays which ranking source is being used instead of a generic label.
description: The rank column on the Roster page now shows the ranking source abbreviation (e.g. Sleep ADP, FBG, KTC) directly in the column header so you always know which rankings you're looking at. The full source name is available on hover.
imageAlt: Roster page showing ranking source abbreviation in the column header

title: Salary Analytics
summary: Explore salary benchmarks by position with starter averages, medians, and full player breakdowns.
description: The Salary Benchmarks page has been upgraded to a full Salary Analytics tool. Every position now shows starter average and starter median based on realistic league starter counts, plus overall average and median salary across all rostered players.

Browse the complete ranked salary list for each position with a Show More button to reveal all players. Use the year selector to compare salary trends across every season back to 2021, and switch between weekly snapshots to see how values shifted throughout the year.
imageAlt: Salary Analytics page with position benchmarks, starter averages, and player salary breakdowns

title: Import Rankings
summary: Import player rankings from FantasyPros, FootballGuys, Sleeper, and more — then drag to set your preferred display order.
description: A new Import Rankings page lets you bring in player rankings from 7 popular fantasy football sites using bookmarklets. Drag a bookmarklet to your bookmarks bar, visit the rankings site, click it, and your rankings are automatically imported.

Drag and drop your saved rankings to control the column order on the Free Agents page. The top ranking in the list becomes the leftmost column. Up to 8 ranking columns display for free agents, or 5 when rostered players are included.

Supported sites include FantasyPros, FootballGuys, Dynasty League Football, KeepTradeCut, Sleeper, Yahoo, and CBS Sports. All data stays private in your browser — never sent to the server or shared with other league members.
imageAlt: Import Rankings page with bookmarklet instructions and draggable ranking source list

title: Free Agent Scouting Tool
summary: Research free agents with ADP rankings, snap counts, and detailed player profiles.
description: A new scouting tool for free agent research is now available. Browse all available players with ADP Redraft and ADP Dynasty rankings side by side, plus NFL snap count data from last season including total snaps and snap percentage.
Filter by position, search by name or team, and toggle rookies in or out. Every column is sortable so you can quickly find undervalued targets or high-usage players heading into the auction.
imageAlt: Free Agents scouting table with player rankings, snap counts, and position filters

title: What's New Page
summary: Track every new feature and improvement in one place.
description: A dedicated changelog page that documents all new features, enhancements, and improvements as they ship. Each entry links to the relevant page so you can try new features immediately.
imageAlt: What's New changelog page showing feature entries with dates and category badges

title: GM/Coach Mode Toggle
summary: Switch between GM and Coach views on your roster page.
description: A new toggle lets you switch between GM mode (salary, contracts, trade value) and Coach mode (matchups, injury status, start/sit decisions). Coach mode is available to all users with a demo experience for visitors.
imageAlt: Roster page in Coach Mode showing matchup data and start/sit columns

title: Trade Bait Marketplace
summary: See which players teams have listed as available for trade.
description: The Trade Builder includes a Trade Bait Marketplace showing players that other owners have flagged as available. During the February transition period, trade bait from both the current and incoming league years is displayed.
imageAlt: Trade Builder page showing the Trade Bait Marketplace with available players

title: League Summary Page
summary: Compare all 16 teams across cap, roster, and contract metrics.
description: A comprehensive comparison dashboard with 14 categories including salary cap usage, roster construction, contract lengths, draft capital, and more. Data spans 5 years for trend analysis.
imageAlt: League Summary dashboard comparing all 16 teams across salary cap and roster metrics

title: Trade Builder
summary: Simulate trades and instantly see salary cap impact for both teams.
description: Build hypothetical trades by selecting players from any two teams. The tool shows cap impact, contract details, and roster changes in real time. Defaults to the two teams with the most cap room.
imageAlt: Trade Builder showing two-team player selection with salary cap impact analysis

title: What's Next Timeline
summary: The homepage now shows your current, next, and upcoming league events.
description: A 3-card timeline at the top of the homepage showing the most relevant league events. Cards display countdown timers, action links, and automatically update as events pass.
imageAlt: Homepage showing the What's Next timeline with three upcoming league event cards

title: League Calendar
summary: View all important league dates and deadlines in one place.
description: A full calendar page showing every league event for the season. Events are color-coded by category with countdown timers for tagging periods, free agency windows, draft dates, trading deadline, and playoffs.
imageAlt: League Calendar page with color-coded events and countdown timers

title: ESPN Player Headshots
summary: Player cards now show real ESPN headshot photos.
description: All player cards across the site now display actual player headshots sourced from ESPN. Avatars are displayed at 150% zoom for a cleaner look.

title: Design System & Dark Mode Foundation
summary: Migrated all styles to CSS custom properties with dark mode support.
description: All SCSS variables have been replaced with native CSS custom properties in a single tokens.css file. This lays the foundation for a dark mode toggle and ensures consistent theming across every component.

title: Custom Football Icon Set
summary: A complete set of custom-designed football icons throughout the app.
description: The navigation drawer and header now use a custom-designed SVG football icon set replacing generic icons. Includes icons for lineups, trades, drafts, standings, and more.

title: Navigation Drawer Redesign
summary: A new collapsible side drawer for faster navigation between pages.
description: The navigation has been completely rebuilt as a slide-out drawer with sections, league switching, team info, and keyboard support. It collapses to a slim icon rail on desktop and overlays on mobile.

title: League Rules & Constitution
summary: The full league constitution is now available on the site.
description: All league rules, scoring settings, roster requirements, and contract details are documented in a searchable, browsable format. No more digging through PDFs or message board posts.
imageAlt: League Rules and Constitution page with searchable rule sections

title: Playoff Brackets
summary: View the full playoff bracket with matchups, results, and prizes.
description: A visual playoff bracket page showing all tournament matchups, winners bracket, consolation rounds, and prize payouts. Updates automatically as the playoffs progress.
imageAlt: Playoff bracket page showing tournament matchups and winners bracket

title: Draft Order Predictor
summary: See the projected draft order for next season based on current standings.
description: Predicts next year's draft order using current season standings, playoff results, and the league's draft order rules. Updates weekly during the season.
imageAlt: Draft Order Predictor showing projected pick order based on current standings

title: Standings Page
summary: View current standings with division, playoff, and all-play views.
description: A complete standings page with multiple views: division standings, league-wide playoff race, and all-play records. Includes win/loss records, points for/against, and tiebreaker details.
imageAlt: Standings page showing division standings with win-loss records and points

title: Team Rosters
summary: View all team rosters with player details, salaries, and contracts.
description: The core roster page showing every team's players with salary, contract length, position, and status. Includes next-year salary projections with the 10% escalation rule applied.
imageAlt: Team Roster page showing player salaries, contract lengths, and position details

title: Salary History Charts
summary: Track how team salary spending has changed across seasons.
description: Interactive charts showing salary cap trends over time. Toggle between total spending, position breakdowns, and individual team views.
imageAlt: Salary History page with interactive charts showing cap spending trends across seasons

title: Salary-Adjusted MVP Rankings
summary: See which players deliver the most value relative to their salary.
description: MVP rankings that factor in player salary to find the best value performers. A $1M player scoring like a $5M player ranks higher than the reverse.
imageAlt: MVP Rankings page showing salary-adjusted value rankings for top performers

title: Salary Analytics
summary: Compare your team's spending against league averages by position.
description: A benchmarking tool that shows how each team's positional spending compares to the league-wide average. Helps identify overpays and value contracts.
imageAlt: Salary Benchmarks page comparing team spending against league averages by position

title: Extension Calculator
summary: Calculate the future cost of contract extensions with 10% annual escalation.
description: A calculator that projects contract costs across multiple years, factoring in the league's 10% annual salary escalation. Essential for planning long-term roster moves.
imageAlt: Extension Calculator showing projected contract costs with 10% annual escalation

title: Asset Library
summary: Browse team logos, banners, and brand graphics for all 16 teams.
description: A gallery of all team visual assets including logos, banners, and icons. Useful for message board posts, fantasy content, and league communications.
imageAlt: Asset Library gallery showing team logos, banners, and brand graphics
